req.title: Read patient (consent)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Consent state at the time of the operation must be tested, including post-revocation timing windows.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-102 governs clinician read access to Patient resources under three distinct conditions: (1) assigned clinician with active consent — happy path, (2) unassigned clinician — access denied, and (3) consent revoked immediately before the read — boundary on post-revocation timing window. EP partitions the space into assigned/unassigned clinician. NEG covers the unassigned path. BVA probes the revocation timing boundary (t=0 after revocation). STC covers the consent state machine: ACTIVE → REVOKED transition and its effect on read access. DT captures the combined decision of assignment × consent-state.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: Clinician has role=clinician and is assigned to the target patient
actions: Authenticate as assigned clinician to obtain JWT
Capture response status, body, and audit log entry
expected: Audit log contains one entry with actor=<CLINICIAN_ID>, resource=Patient/<PATIENT_ID>, action=read, outcome=success
trace: R-HLT-102: clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit(
    assigned_clinician_token, patient_id, audit_log_client
):
    resp = <api_get>(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", token=assigned_clinician_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == patient_id

    entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(resource=f"Patient/{patient_id}", action="read")
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    latest = entries[0]
    assert latest["outcome"] == "success"
    assert latest["actor"] == <CLINICIAN_ID>

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Clinician has role=clinician but is NOT assigned to the target patient
actions: Authenticate as unassigned clinician to obtain JWT
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code == 'forbidden'
No patient data is present in the response body
trace: R-HLT-102: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403(
    unassigned_clinician_token, patient_id
):
    resp = <api_get>(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", token=unassigned_clinician_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("code") == "forbidden" for i in issues)
    assert "birthDate" not in body
    assert "name" not in body

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_immediately_after_consent_revocation_returns_403
preconditions: Clinician has role=clinician and is assigned to the target patient
Patient consent state transitions from ACTIVE to REVOKED at time T
actions: Authenticate as assigned clinician to obtain JWT
Revoke patient consent via consent administration endpoint at time T
Immediately (delta < 1s) HTTP GET /Patient/<PATIENT_ID> with Authorization: Bearer <token>
Capture response status, body, and audit log entry
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden returned at or after T+0
Response body contains OperationOutcome indicating consent revoked
Audit log records read attempt with outcome=failure and reason=consent-revoked
trace: R-HLT-102: consent state at the time of the operation must be tested
R-HLT-102: 403 when clinician not assigned (by consent revocation)
tests.executable:
import time
import pytest

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_immediately_after_consent_revocation_returns_403(
    assigned_clinician_token, patient_id, consent_admin_token, audit_log_client
):
    rev_resp = <api_post>(
        f"/Consent/{patient_id}/revoke",
        token=consent_admin_token,
        json={"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "patient-request"}
    )
    assert rev_resp.status_code in (200, 204), "Consent revocation setup failed"

    resp = <api_get>(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", token=assigned_clinician_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any("consent" in i.get("diagnostics", "").lower() for i in issues)

    entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(resource=f"Patient/{patient_id}", action="read")
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    latest = entries[0]
    assert latest["outcome"] == "failure"
    assert "consent" in latest.get("reason", "").lower()

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_read_denied_after_consent_active_to_revoked_state_transition
preconditions: Clinician has role=clinician and is assigned to the target patient
actions: Authenticate as assigned clinician to obtain JWT
Verify read succeeds while consent is ACTIVE (pre-transition)
Revoke patient consent — state transitions ACTIVE → REVOKED
expected: Both attempts are captured in audit log with correct outcomes
trace: R-HLT-102: consent state at the time of the operation must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_assigned_clinician_read_denied_after_consent_active_to_revoked_state_transition(
    assigned_clinician_token, patient_id, consent_admin_token, audit_log_client
):
    pre = <api_get>(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", token=assigned_clinician_token)
    assert pre.status_code == 200, "Expected 200 while consent is ACTIVE"

    rev = <api_post>(
        f"/Consent/{patient_id}/revoke",
        token=consent_admin_token,
        json={"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "patient-request"}
    )
    assert rev.status_code in (200, 204)

    post = <api_get>(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", token=assigned_clinician_token)
    assert post.status_code == 403, "Expected 403 after consent REVOKED"

    entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(resource=f"Patient/{patient_id}", action="read", limit=2)
    outcomes = [e["outcome"] for e in entries]
    assert "success" in outcomes
    assert "failure" in outcomes

tests.name: test_decision_table_assignment_and_consent_combinations
preconditions: Two clinicians exist: assigned_clinician and unassigned_clinician
Consent states tested: ACTIVE and REVOKED
actions: For each combination of (assigned|unassigned) x (active|revoked) consent, issue GET /Patient/<PATIENT_ID>
Record HTTP status code for each combination
trace: R-HLT-102: clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care
R-HLT-102: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
R-HLT-102: consent state at the time of the operation must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("token_fixture,consent_state,expected_status", [
    ("assigned_clinician_token",   "active",   200),
    ("assigned_clinician_token",   "revoked",  403),
    ("unassigned_clinician_token", "active",   403),
    ("unassigned_clinician_token", "revoked",  403),
])
def test_decision_table_assignment_and_consent_combinations(
    request, token_fixture, consent_state, expected_status,
    patient_id, consent_admin_token
):
    token = request.getfixturevalue(token_fixture)
    <set_consent_state>(patient_id, consent_state, admin_token=consent_admin_token)
    resp = <api_get>(f"/Patient/{patient_id}", token=token)
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"assignment={token_fixture}, consent={consent_state}: "
        f"expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
